violations.description: Coverage is heavily skewed toward static regex checks of workflow/config text, with limited runtime execution-path validation.
violations.suggestion: Add at least one execution-oriented CI contract test that evaluates job graph behavior from resolved workflow data.
violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] defines required policy-first quality-stage jobs ...')

violations.description: Current tests strongly validate blocker conditions but do not explicitly simulate a full all-green merge-ready path for all required gates together.
violations.suggestion: Add a happy-path release-readiness test that verifies policy/lint/test/burn-in/quality-gates complete and status resolves ready.

violations.description: Operator journey checks are primarily script/doc assertions rather than end-user UI interaction flows.
violations.suggestion: Introduce one targeted UI flow assertion for rollout control visibility/diagnostics where applicable.
violations.code_snippet: test('[E6-AUTOMATE-E2E-203][P1] scheduled burn-in workflow keeps docs-only PR skip behavior ...')

untested_user_paths: All-green merge-ready aggregate path with all required gate successes in one scenario

recommendations: Add one merge-ready happy-path integration check across all gates.
Balance text/regex policy assertions with at least one execution-path contract check.
Preserve strong blocker-path coverage while adding confidence on success-path semantics.
summary: Coverage is good for blocker and guardrail detection, with moderate gaps in runtime semantics and full success-path simulation.
